MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR JAN-24-2022 -- APPEARANCES BY BITA AZIMI, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF MARVIN VALENCIA; JOHN CARR, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT CHAMPION SCAFFOLD SERVICES -- SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $250.00 EACH, PAYABLE BY FEB-24-2022, ARE IMPOSED ON ATTY. BITA AZIMI AND ATTY. JOHN CARR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER PURSUANT TO THE NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL AND TRIAL ORDER. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE ARE SET ON FEB-25-2022 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. OSC WILL BE DISCHARGED IF SANCTIONS ARE PAID PRIOR TO THE HEARING DATE. PARTIES TO REACH OUT TO SETTLEMENT OFFICER MOROZUMI FOR FURTHER SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. IF THE CASE DOES NOT SETTLE, PARTIES ORDERED TO RETURN FOR TRIAL CALL ON MAR-30-2022 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. PARTIES WAIVE NOTICE OF THE TRIAL DATE. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: JIROQUE; MATTER NOT REPORTED (206)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication; **Court Reporter Fees Paid**
Off Calendar Oct-29-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. BITA M. AZIMI, Esq. of BD&J (Tel: 310-887-1818) for plaintiff Marvin Valencia (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. JOHN N. CARR, Esq. of Kidd Carr LLP (Tel: 310-268-8600) for defendant Champion Scaffold Services, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CHAMPION SCAFFOLD SERVICES, INC.'s Motion for Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Champion Scaffold Services, Inc.'s ("Champion") motion for summary judgment is denied. Champion's motion for summary adjudication is granted on the premises liability cause of action as conceded. The motion for summary adjudication is denied on the negligence claim. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Champion breached its duty to provide overhead protection and whether that breach proximately caused injury to plaintiff.

Plaintiff's employer (AC Enterprises) retained Champion to "Furnish, set up & dismantle scaffolding." (Cruz Decl., Ex. A.) The subcontract does not obligate Champion to inspect the scaffolding and such inspection duties were delegated to AC Enterprises. AC Enterprises' employee would daily inspect the scaffolding. (Carr Decl., Ex. D [Deposition of Salvador Fuentes, 19:13-20:17], Ex. E [Deposition of Javier Molina, 21:15-19, 49:17-25].)

AC Enterprises installed plywood protection board on the scaffolding to prevent debris and tools from falling on those below. (Carr Decl., Ex. D [Deposition of Salvador Fuentes, 23:23-24:3].) On December 27, 2017, plaintiff was injured when he fell through the plywood protection board. (Carr Decl., Ex. D [Deposition of Salvador Fuentes 40:4-17, 58:16-21, 75:22-76:1].)

8 CCR 1637(q) provides: "Overhead Protection. Workers on scaffolds who are exposed to overhead hazards shall be provided with overhead protection or other means that will effectively eliminate the hazard." Based on plaintiff's expert testimony, there is a dispute of fact whether Champion breached this duty and there is no evidence that the duty to provide overhead protection was delegated to AC Enterprises (if it is in fact a delegable duty). "Defendant failed to provide adequate overhead protection for their scaffold, prompting the plywood protection board to be installed on the scaffold, ultimately leading to Mr. Valencia's fall and subsequent injuries." (Moore Decl., par. 9.) In sum, there is a dispute of fact regarding whether Champion properly erected the scaffolding.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
